'''Nathan Peats''' (born 5 October 1990) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Tugun Seahawks]] in the [[Gold Coast Rugby League]].

Peats previously played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He has played at representative level for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]], [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]] and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]].

==Background==
Peats was born in [[Camperdown, New South Wales]], Australia and is of [[Greeks|Greek]] and [[Indigenous Australian]] descent. Peats is eligible to play for Greece, the son of a former [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] player, [[Geordi Peats]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/eels/nathan-peats-father-geordi-attacks-eels-board-over-salary-cap-scandal/news-story/01713072ad55a04dadef7538b8ec9f63|title=Peats' dad blasts 'pathetic' Eels board}}</ref>

Peats played his junior football for the [[La Perouse United|La Perouse Panthers]], going through the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] junior ranks and attended [[Christian Brothers' High School, Lewisham]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/it-was-time-to-give-back-peats/tabid/10874/newsid/95152/default.aspx|title=It was time to give back: Peats|date=22 April 2016}}</ref>

==Club career==
Peats played in South Sydney's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2009–2010 where he even captained the team in 2010 helping the team make it into the 2010 Toyota Cup Grand Final against the Junior [[New Zealand Warriors]] but lost 42–28.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2010-10-03/young-warriors-down-souths/2283308|title=Young Warriors down Souths|publisher=ABC News|location=Australia|date=3 October 2010}}</ref>

===2011===
In round 4, Peats made his NRL debut for South Sydney against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], off the interchange bench in the 32–30 win at [[Central Coast Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.perthnow.com.au/sport/nrl/souths-hold-off-fast-finishing-manly/news-story/e0be5276f4e12de305ac2548f9a6e5e7|title=Bunnies on the hop|date=April 2011}}</ref> In Round 21 against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], Peats scored his first NRL try in the comeback 34–24 win after being down 20–0 after 35 minutes at [[WIN Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/south-sydney-rabbitohs-fight-back-from-20-0-deficit-to-beat-st-george-illawarra-dragons-34-24-at-win-stadium/news-story/bdae9691dc2f7289532ac933c8fc4a93|title=Bunnies fight back from dead}}</ref> On 28 August, Peats re-signed with the South Sydney club until the end of the 2013 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/tyrrell-mcqueen-peats-resign-rabbitohs-2013-2205/|title=Tyrrell, McQueen and Peats re-sign with Rabbitohs – Zero Tackle|first=Zero|last=Tackle|date=27 May 2011}}</ref> He finished his debut year playing in 20 matches and scoring a try in the [[2011 NRL season]].

===2012===
[[File:NATHAN PEATS.jpg|thumb|right|Peats playing for the South Sydney Rabbitohs in 2012]] 
On 4 February, Peats played for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] against the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl-all-stars-win-arthur-beetson-trophy-in-thriller/news-story/fc6859fafb29b6141871d1b25e3f3859|title=NRL All-Stars win in thriller|date=4 February 2012}}</ref> After South Sydney's 20–12 loss against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in round 4, Peats was dropped to Souths [[New South Wales Cup|NSW Cup]] team, the [[North Sydney Bears]], where he played round 15.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydneys-nathan-peats-saves-room-for-nrl-premiership-tattoo/news-story/ca45f4328de474844b854e22148f4301|title=Peats leaves ink space for title tat}}</ref> After round 15, Peats remained his spot in the top 17 for the rest of the season, starting at hooker in most of the matches. Peats finished the [[2012 NRL season]] with him playing in 18 matches and scoring five tries.

===2013===
Peats again played for the Indigenous All Stars in the pre-season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-indigenous-all-stars-v-nrl-all-stars-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/20ebc0457af6f07bee1ca72477e6e0e8|title=Indigenous All Stars win 32–6|publisher=News.com.au |date=9 February 2013|last1=Burke|first1=Dominic}}</ref> On 13 March, he re-signed with the South Sydney club until the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2013/03/13/rabbitohs-re-sign-nathan-peats-for-seasons-2014-and-2015.html|title=Rabbitohs Re-sign Nathan Peats for Seasons 2014 and 2015|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=13 March 2013}}</ref> On 21 April, Peats played for the [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City Origin]] team against the [[Country Origin New South Wales team|Country Origin]] team where he started at hooker in the 12–18 loss at [[Coffs Harbour, New South Wales|Coffs Harbour]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/adam-reynolds-shows-his-origin-style-but-daley-wont-come-knocking/news-story/3cb25246ee4e61cd5ac770049a87b1cc|title=Unlucky Reynolds shows Origin style|date=21 April 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/how-they-rated-city-origin-20130421-2i8js.html|title=How they rated: City Origin|first=Brad|last=Walter|date=21 April 2013}}</ref> On 24 June, three months after signing a contract extension with the club, Peats went on to sign a three-year deal with the [[Parramatta Eels]] starting in 2014. Peats commented on his signing saying, "it was a tough decision, the biggest of my life. I'm a Souths junior and love the club to death but to further my career I believe it's what's best for me, There is a long way to go before the season is over and I'm fully committed to Souths and having a year to remember".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/peats-makes-tough-decision-to-leave-rabbitohs-for-eels-20130723-2qhhx.html|title=Peats makes tough decision to leave Rabbitohs for Eels|first=Michael|last=Chammas|date=24 July 2013|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> 
[[File:Nathan Peats - Man Of Steel Premiere Sydney.jpg|thumb|right|Peats at the [[Man of Steel (film)|Man of Steel]] premiere in Sydney in 2013]] 
After round 22, where the South Sydney side lost 26–8 against the [[Melbourne Storm]], Peats was demoted to the [[NSW Cup]] and didn't feature in any more matches for the South Sydney club for the remainder of the season. He played in 17 matches in the [[2013 NRL season]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2014===
In February, Peats was selected in the Eels [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=NRL – The official site of the National Rugby League – NRL.com|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016}}</ref> In round 1, he made his debut for Parramatta, against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], playing at hooker in the 36–16 win at [[Parramatta Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/hattrick-heroes-semi-radradra-vai-toutai-inspire-electric-eels-to-win-over-warriors/news-story/667069418cafe2db4adf9887b63c738a|title=Hat-trick heroes lead Eels to victory|date=10 March 2014}}</ref> In round 2, against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], Peats scored his first try for Parramatta in the 18–22 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-03-23/sea-eagles-steal-last-minute-win-over-eels/5339624|title=Sea Eagles steal last-minute win over Eels|date=23 March 2014}}</ref> After good early season form, his name was thrown up with others as a potential replacement for the injured [[Robbie Farah]] at hooker for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/parramattas-nathan-peats-rockets-into-origin-contention-following-robbie-farahs-injury/news-story/fe9166acef018367a078c86feb375c9c|title=Peats rockets into Origin frame|date=14 April 2014}}</ref> Unfortunately, in round 12 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Peats suffered a season ending [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/eels/parramatta-eels-nine-nightmare-with-hooker-nathan-peats-season-ending-injury/news-story/08fc7c07cea8bf0a08a558d1f2decaf0|title=Eels nine nightmare with Peats injured}}</ref> Despite his season being cut short early, Peats was branded "one of the best buys of the year".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/acl-injury-ends-nathan-peats-season-20140531-zru17.html|title=ACL injury ends Nathan Peats' season|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=31 May 2014|access-date=1 May 2017|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> He finished his [[2014 NRL season|first year]] for the Parramatta club with 10 matches and three tries.

===2015===
On 3 May, Peats returned as hooker for City Origin.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/country-defeat-city-34-22/tabid/10874/newsid/85906/default.aspx|title=Country overcome City in seesawing affair}}</ref> In round 23, against the [[Sydney Roosters]], he suffered a season ending neck injury in the 18–28 loss at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nathan-peats-to-undergo-spinal-surgery-with-eels-confident-he-will-recover-for-2016-season/news-story/b08847fbb427adbb81e856b68462f2de|title=Peats 'will make full recovery from broken neck'}}</ref> He scored four tries in his 19 matches for the Parramatta side.

===2016===
On 8 May, Peats made his third appearance at hooker for the City Origin team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/aaron-gray-scores-four-tries-to-put-his-name-up-in-lights-as-city-origin-upset-country-origin-in-high-scorer/news-story/ec11f0e7f9bbafde14cf806ad9b46e6a|title=Four-try Gray the surprise star in City rout|date=8 May 2016}}</ref> On 11 May, following the [[Parramatta Eels salary cap breach]], Peats joined the [[Gold Coast Titans]] effective immediately on a contract to the end of 2017, after he was asked to leave to help get the Parramatta club back under the salary cap.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/parramatta-eels/parramatta-eels-hooker-nathan-peats-to-gold-coast-titans-deal-all-but-sealed-20160511-gosw6y.html|title=Parramatta Eels to play for points after hooker Nathan Peats signs with Gold Coast Titans|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=11 May 2016|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> Peats finished his time with Parramatta in the middle of the season, playing in six matches and scoring a try.  After being asked to leave by Parramatta, Peats spoke to the media about his displeasure with the club by saying "The only regret I probably have is with myself and the things I've done during the games, "I've got my body in the wrong spot a few times for the club, for myself and for the boys and I regret that at times because now look at it, I'm the one walking away, It's been a poorly run business to be honest in the last couple of years ... until they sort that stuff out I think it's going to keep continuing, No-one's going to say sorry so it is what it is, I don't really care. I'm looking forward to moving on. What's a sorry going to do?"<ref>[http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nathan-peats-has-one-regret-from-his-time-at-the-eels/news-story/ccdd5baafa0cbe803384e07428ff59d2 Nathan Peats regrets putting his body on the line for Parramatta Eels | news.com.au <!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

In round 11, Peats made his debut for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]], playing off the interchange bench and scoring a try in the 28–24 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/tyrone-peachey-brainsnap-costs-penrith-panthers-against-gold-coast-titans-20160522-gp0zxz.html|title=Tyrone Peachey brainsnap costs Penrith Panthers against Gold Coast Titans|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> He scored two tries in his 14 matches for the Gold Coast.

===2017===
On 10 February 2016, Peats started at hooker for the Indigenous All Stars against the [[World All Stars]] in the 34–8 win at [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-thump-world-all-stars-348-in-newcastle/news-story/a016f102b1c7ddc5bbdeeedb83a149c5|title=Indigenous All Stars too hot to handle}}</ref> and made his State of Origin debut on 31 May for the NSW Blues at Suncorp Stadium against the Queensland Maroons. Peats re-signed with the Gold Coast outfit until the end of the 2020 season.

===2018===
Peats made 20 appearances for the Gold Coast in 2018 as the club missed out on the finals finishing in 14th position.  Peats also missed out on origin selection in 2018 with coach [[Brad Fittler]] opting to select [[Damien Cook]] as hooker.<ref>[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NzQtMTcxNy00LS0tLS0tMTY4ODctLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t Custom Match List – Rugby League Project<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref><ref>[https://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/titans-lose-star-hooker-for-extended-period-peats-to-lose-origin-jumper/news-story/88a382808bc12533cb89c1566d92610c No Cookies | Gold Coast Bulletin<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/08/gold-coast-titans-2018-season-review/|title=Gold Coast Titans 2018 season review|publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref>

===2019===
In round 24 2019, Peats played his 150th NRL game in the Gold Coast's 38–4 loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]] at [[Newcastle International Sports Centre|McDonald Jones Stadium]].  Peats made a total of 13 appearances for the Gold Coast in 2019 as the club endured a horror year on and off the field winning only 4 matches all season and claiming the wooden spoon after finishing last.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.adelaidenow.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-titans-signings-contact-jai-arrow-ash-taylor-mal-meninga-paul-kent/news-story/c3f13d1c4729944c04718c59244b0574/|title=The Titans are full of 'hot air'. Their latest blunder proves it|website=The Advertiser|location=Adelaide}}</ref>

===2020===
Peats played eight games for the Gold Coast in the [[2020 NRL season]].  Following the conclusion of the season, Peats was released by the Gold Coast.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/nrl-2020-nrl-contract-news-signings-transfers-tyrone-roberts-leaves-gold-coast-titans/news-story/fc243b6b9be9fa4e9cf98cd4c2e8d616/|title= Former captain Tyrone Roberts shown the door by Gold Coast Titans|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia}}</ref>

===2021===
On 22 March 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=BBC Sport|date=22 March 2021|access-date=22 March 2021|title=Nathan Peats: Leigh Centurions sign ex-New South Wales State of Origin hooker|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56486962}}</ref>
On 11 July 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on a season-long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 July 2021|access-date=11 July 2021|title=Leigh confirm player-swap deals with Huddersfield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-confirm-player-swap-deals-with-huddersfield/}}</ref>
In round 15 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored his first try for Huddersfield in a 40–26 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-hull/55652/|title= Four tries from Jermaine McGillvary help Huddersfield Giants to 40–26 win over Hull FC|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref>
On 18 November 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Racing Club Albi XIII]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 November 2021|accessdate=24 December 2021|title=Nathan Peats makes move to France|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nathan-peats-makes-move-to-france/}}</ref>

===2022===
On 27 May, Peats signed a two-year deal to join [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-confirm-signing-of-australian-hooker-nathan-peats/|title=Toulouse confirm signing of Australian hooker Nathan Peats|website=loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
In late 2022 after the club was relegated from the Super League, Peats requested a release from his Toulouse Olympique contract to join [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] ahead of the 2023 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-nathan-peats-rl-28764331/|title=Huddersfield Giants' Nathan Peats feels at "home" again after crazy 19 months of moves|website=mirror.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
On 20 September, Peats announced he would be retiring from rugby league at the end of the 2023 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/all-good-things-must-come-to-an-end-nathan-peats-becomes-latest-huddersfield-giants-player-to-announce-retirement-4342119/|title='All good things must come to an end': Nathan Peats becomes latest Huddersfield Giants player to announce retirement|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

==International==
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]], he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Post-playing career==
In December 2024 it was report he had taken the role of Community Development Officer at [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 Dec 2024|title=Former Super League and State of Origin star lands off-field role Down Under for 2025|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-and-state-of-origin-star-lands-off-field-role-down-under-for-2025}}</ref>
